pattern: Since repeats never add extra value, reducing the string to its set of distinct characters first turns the whole thing into a simple category-lookup-and-sum.
hints: A repeated character only ever contributes once, so start by collapsing the password down to its distinct characters.
Each distinct character falls into exactly one of four categories — lowercase letter, uppercase letter, digit, or one of the four special symbols — each worth a fixed number of points.
Sum the point value for every distinct character's category; there is nothing more to combine across categories.

edge_inputs.description: heavy repetition of the same characters, each should count only once
every distinct special character present at once
a mix designed so naive per-character summing (without deduplicating) would overcount

stress.description: random passwords drawn from the full allowed character set, across the full length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789!@#$'
    password = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [password]
